author: Augustine of Hippo
book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
How that Pelagius Deceived the Synod of Palestine.
title: How that Pelagius Deceived the Synod of Palestine.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: Chapter 11 [X.]—How that Pelagius Deceived the Synod of Palestine.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: Now I pray you carefully to observe by what evidence Pelagius is shown to have deceived his judges in Palestine, not to mention other points, on this very question of the baptism of infants, lest we should seem to any one to have used calumny and suspicion, rather than to have ascertained the certain fact, when we alleged that Pelagius concealed the opinion which Cœlestius expressed with greater frankness, while at the same time he actually entertained the same views.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: Now, from what has been stated above, it has been clearly seen that Cœlestius refused to condemn the assertion that “Adam’s sin injured only himself, and not the human race, and that infants at their birth are in the same state that Adam was before the transgression,” because he saw that, if he condemned these propositions, he would affirm that there was in infants a transmission of sin from Adam.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: When, however, it was objected to Pelagius that he was of one mind with Cœlestius on this point, he condemned the words without hesitation.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: I am quite aware that you have read all this before.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: Since, however, we are not writing this account for you alone, we proceed to transcribe the very words of the synodal acts, lest the reader should be unwilling either to turn to the record for himself, or if he does not possess it, take the trouble to procure a copy.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
